data.title: VOO vs. VTI: Is the Total Market ETF Riskier Than the S&P 500?
data.description:
When investors want simple, low-cost exposure to the U.S. stock market, two Vanguard ETFs usually dominate the conversation: the Vanguard S&P 500 ETF (VOO) and the Vanguard Total Stock Market ETF (VTI). At first glance, the two funds look almost identical. Both charge the same ultra-low 0.03% expense ratio, have similar top holdings, and offer a dividend yield of around 1.13%. However, the real difference comes down to diversification and market exposure. VOO may offer a slightly smoother ride because of its focus on established large-cap leaders. VTI, on the other hand, provides broader diversification and the potential for stronger long-term risk-reward by capturing the full U.S. equity market.

Using the TipRanks' ETF Comparison Tool, we compared VOO and VTI on multiple parameters. Below is a screenshot.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: Tesla (TSLA) Gets Go-Ahead for UK Home Power Supply — A Bullish Spark for the Stock?
data.description:
Tesla (TSLA) is now one step closer to its goal of supplying British homes with electricity. On Thursday, the UK's energy regulator confirmed that it has approved the U.S. electric vehicle maker's license application.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The move did not move investors, as Tesla's shares continued to flirt with the red zone in early Thursday trading. However, the approval comes as Tesla continues to see patchy results in its efforts to reverse sales decline in Europe.

This includes the UK, where the automaker sold 37% fewer vehicles last month compared to the same period in 2025. Sales also fell in the Netherlands and Denmark but rose in France, Norway, and Spain, for instance.

Tesla Energy Ventures, Tesla's retail electricity supply arm, applied in July to the Office of Gas and Electricity Markets (Ofgem) to enter the UK energy supply market last year — a move that could see it take on market leaders such as Centrica (GB:CNA), the owner of British Gas, and Octopus Energy.

In addition, the move could provide a boost for the company, considering its existing infrastructure advantage and the fact that its subsidiary, Tesla Motors Limited, already holds an electricity generation license granted by Ofgem in 2020. Moreover, the automaker's energy arm, Tesla Energy, already sells solar technology and battery storage units in Europe's second-largest market.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: Morgan Stanley Reaffirms 'Sell' on Rivian Stock Ahead of Today's R2 Launch
data.description:
Electric vehicle maker Rivian Automotive (RIVN) is set to unveil the launch edition of its R2 vehicle today, March 12. The smaller and lower-priced model could help Rivian reach more buyers and increase production in the years ahead. However, top Morgan Stanley analyst Andrew Percoco remains cautious on the stock.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Percoco kept an Underweight rating on the stock with a $12 price target, implying 23% downside from current levels. While he said the R2 launch is important for Rivian, he warned the company still faces big hurdles as it tries to ramp up production and move toward profits.

Morgan Stanley's Take on Rivian

According to the report, the upcoming R2 vehicle is an important step for Rivian as it tries to scale its auto business. Management said the model could help drive higher production and improve operating leverage across the company.

Morgan Stanley also highlighted Rivian's push to build more of its technology in-house. The company is developing its own chip, called RAP1, which is expected to power advanced driver-assist and future self-driving features in the R2 platform.

Even so, the firm prefers to remain on the sidelines for now. Morgan Stanley expects 2026 to be a transition year for Rivian as it works toward positive gross margins and ramps production of the new platform. Investors will likely watch closely how the R2 launch affects production scale, demand, and Rivian's path toward profitability.

Is RIVN Stock a Buy or Sell?   

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on RIVN stock based on eight Buys, eight Holds, and six Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average RIVN price target of $17.32 per share implies 9.14% upside potential.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Dow Jones Shrugs Off Encouraging Jobless Claims Data
data.description:
The Dow Jones (DJIA) is set to open lower on Thursday following the release of new jobs data from the Department of Labor (DOL).

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

For the week ended March 7, initial jobless claims remained unchanged at 213,000, below the consensus estimate of 215,000. The previous week's claims data was revised lower by 1,000 to 213,000. Continuing jobless claims, which trail initial claims by a week, fell by 21,000 to 1.85 million and were slightly above the expectation of 1.849 million.

*This is a developing story. Stay tuned for additional updates.*
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Why Did Decent Holding Inc. Stock (DXST) Plummet Today?
data.description:
Decent Holding Inc. (DXST) stock fell hard on Thursday after the Chinese industrial wastewater treatment services company announced a reverse stock split. The company said that it will consolidate shares on a one-for-25 basis. This will affect the company's Class A and Class B shares, which will start trading on a split-adjusted basis on March 16, 2026. The stock will still trade under the DXST ticker but will switch to the new CUSIP number G2748R205.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Decent Holding Inc. noted that the reverse stock split will boost the price of the company's shares. This is the main goal of this action, as the company needs to push its stock price above the $1 minimum required to maintain a listing on the Nasdaq exchange.

The fact that Decent Holding Inc. has announced a reverse stock split to maintain its Nasdaq listing already explains why the stock is down today. Investor morale likely took a beating on news that the company had to consolidate shares to continue the listing. This is often seen as a last resort for companies that have no other way of increasing their share price above the $1 minimum.

Decent Holding Inc. Stock Tumbles Today

Decent Holding Inc. stock was down 33.41% in pre-market trading on Thursday, following a 10.91% rally yesterday. The shares have decreased 68.19% year-to-date and 56.23% over the past 12 months.

DXST stock trading activity is elevated today, as more than 35 million shares changed hands, compared to a three-month average daily trading volume of about 46,000 units.
data.author: William White

data.title: 'Deal After Deal': Uber Now Adds Nissan to Its Growing Robotaxi Network
data.description:
Uber (UBER), the global ride-hailing company, is rapidly expanding its push into robotaxis. The company said it will work with Nissan Motor (NSANY) and British self-driving startup Wayve to develop autonomous taxis that riders could eventually book through the Uber app.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The companies plan to prepare a pilot program in Tokyo by late 2026. The program would use Nissan Leaf electric vehicles equipped with Wayve's autonomous driving technology, which would be offered to customers through Uber's platform.

The agreement adds another partner to Uber's growing list of companies building driverless vehicles for its network.

Tokyo Pilot Marks the Next Step

Under the partnership, Nissan will provide the vehicles while Wayve supplies the autonomous driving system. Uber will integrate the service into its ride-hailing app, allowing users to request the robotaxis like a normal ride.

The pilot program is expected to begin preparations over the next two years, with Tokyo selected as the first testing ground.

Uber has increasingly focused on working with outside technology partners instead of building its own autonomous vehicles. This strategy allows the company to expand robotaxi services without taking on the heavy research and development costs.

Another Robotaxi Deal Announced the Same Day

The Nissan partnership was announced the same day Uber revealed another major autonomous vehicle deal. The company said it will partner with Amazon (AMZN)-owned Zoox to bring fully driverless robotaxis to the Uber app. The service is expected to launch in Las Vegas this summer, with expansion to Los Angeles planned by mid-2027.

Analysts believe the deal strengthens Uber's broader autonomous strategy. TD Cowen analyst John Blackledge recently reiterated a Buy rating and a $114 price target on Uber, noting that partnerships like Zoox could help the company facilitate autonomous rides in up to 15 cities globally by the end of 2026.

With agreements now spanning companies such as Zoox, Waymo, May Mobility, Baidu (BIDU), and Wayve, Uber appears to be building a wide network of partners as it prepares for a future where more rides are handled by autonomous vehicles.

Is Uber a Good Stock to Buy Now?

On TipRanks, Uber has a Strong Buy consensus based on 29 Buy, two Hold, and one Sell ratings. It has the highest price target of $150. UBER stock's consensus price target is $105.59, implying a 40.84% upside.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Decline as Oil Prices Continue to Rise amid Iran War
data.description:
U.S. stock futures fell on Thursday morning as rising oil prices amid the escalating Iran conflict impacted investor sentiment. Energy Secretary Chris Wright's announcement that the U.S. will release 172 million barrels of oil from the Strategic Petroleum Reserve and the International Energy Agency's (IEA) decision to release 400 million barrels from emergency reserves failed to bring down oil prices as Iran continues to disrupt supply. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 0.63%, 0.66%, and 0.81%, respectively, at 8:30 a.m. EST on March 12.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

At the time of writing, the price of the global oil benchmark Brent (CM:BZ) was up more than 7% to $98.63 a barrel, while West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude (CM:CL) rose 7% to trade at $93.41 per barrel. Iran continues to strike foreign ships in the Persian Gulf and has warned that oil prices could climb to $200 a barrel.

During Wednesday's regular trading, the three major indexes closed mixed as traders digested developments in the U.S.-Iran war. The S&P 500 and the Dow Jones fell 0.08% and 0.61%, respectively, while the Nasdaq Composite gained 0.08%.

Turning to trending stocks, Dick's Sporting Goods (DKS) shares were up about 5% in Thursday's pre-market trading after the retailer reported a better-than-anticipated holiday quarter. However, the company issued a weak earnings outlook, reflecting the impact of the Foot Locker acquisition. Hims & Hers Health (HIMS) stock surged 5% after Eli Lilly (LLY) issued a notice about the risks associated with compounded tirzepatide mixed with vitamin B12. 

Meanwhile, UiPath (PATH) stock fell more than 9% as the automation software company's Q1 guidance failed to impress investors. In contrast, Bumble (BMBL) shares rallied 21% in pre-market trading after the dating app reported solid Q4 results.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Oil Prices May Climb to $200 Per Barrel after New Tanker Attacks — 5 Energy Stocks to Watch Now
data.description:
Oil prices are surging again as the conflict in the Middle East threatens one of the world's most important energy routes. Iran continues to strike foreign ships in the Persian Gulf and has warned that oil prices could climb to $200 a barrel. These new strikes on ships in or near the Strait of Hormuz have rattled markets and sparked fears of a major supply shock.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

At the time of writing, the price of the global oil benchmark Brent (CM:BZ) was up about 7% to $98.08 a barrel, while West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude (CM:CL) rose more than 6% to trade at $92.50 per barrel.

The stakes are high. The Strait of Hormuz is the most critical oil shipping route in the world, carrying roughly 20% of global crude supply. Any disruption there can tighten supply almost instantly and send prices sharply higher. With markets now pricing in the risk of a prolonged disruption, oil traders are once again bracing for a potential energy shock.

Emergency Oil Release Fails to Ease Supply Fears

Governments have also tried to calm markets. On March 11, the International Energy Agency said its 32 member countries will release 400 million barrels from emergency reserves, including 172 million barrels from the United States.

However, analysts warned that this is a temporary measure. The reserves cannot fully replace the large volume of oil that normally moves through the Strait of Hormuz each day if the disruption continues.

5 Energy Stocks Investors Are Watching as Oil Surges

With oil prices suddenly back above $100, attention is shifting to the companies that produce it. When crude spikes, large oil producers and drilling companies often move higher as the value of their output rises.

  • Exxon Mobil (XOM): Exxon Mobil, the largest publicly traded U.S. oil producer, is often one of the first stocks investors watch when crude prices jump. The company operates across the full energy chain, from oil production to refining and chemicals, which allows it to capture more profit when energy prices rise. On Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on XOM, with analysts projecting about 49% upside.
  • Chevron (CVX): A major global producer with significant assets in the U.S. and around the world. Chevron's stock is highly sensitive to crude price movements, often serving as a primary vehicle for investors seeking direct exposure to oil rallies. According to analysts, CVX has a Moderate Buy rating and roughly 1.02% downside.
  • Occidental Petroleum (OXY): Known for its massive footprint in the Permian Basin, one of the most productive shale regions in the U.S. Occidental is well-positioned to see immediate cash flow benefits when oil prices are elevated. At $52, OXY's average price target suggests about 6.44% downside from current levels.
  • SLB (SLB): This is a leading oilfield services provider. Instead of producing oil itself, SLB provides the technology and expertise others need to extract it. When oil prices are high, producers often ramp up exploration and drilling, which drives demand for SLB's specialized services. On Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on SLB, with analysts projecting about 12.7% upside.
  • EOG Resources (EOG): A top-tier U.S. shale operator recognized for its operational efficiency. EOG's profitability is tightly correlated with market prices for crude, making it a key stock to watch during periods of high oil volatility. At $135.35, EOG's average price target suggests about 2.14% upside from current levels.

Which Is the Best Oil Stock to Buy?

TipRanks Best Oil Stocks tool shows oil field services giant Schlumberger currently makes for the best investment.

Investors can dig deeper to decide which energy stock best fits their strategy. Below is a screenshot for reference.
data.author: Shalu Saraf
